summary: MongoDB persistence for PKO Poker tournaments verified. All 11 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend UI working correctly - tournament creation, registration, add bots, leave & refund all functional. Tournament data survives backend restart (verified via my-tournament endpoint). One bug found: GET /tournaments/{id} returns 404 after restart because it doesn't call initialize().

minor.issue: Returns 404 after backend restart because it doesn't call tournament_manager.initialize() - tournaments not loaded from MongoDB
minor.fix: Add 'await tournament_manager.initialize()' at start of get_tournament endpoint

minor.issue: Frontend logs 'Unknown message type: bot_added' - message type not handled in frontend

passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/poker/tournaments/create - creates and saves to MongoDB
Backend: POST /api/poker/tournaments/register - adds player and saves to MongoDB
Backend: POST /api/poker/tournaments/{id}/add-bots - adds bots and saves to MongoDB
Backend: POST /api/poker/tournaments/force-leave - removes player from DB mapping
Backend: GET /api/poker/my-tournament - returns tournament from memory/DB
Backend: GET /api/poker/tournaments - shows all open tournaments from DB
Backend: Tournament state persists after player actions
Backend: Multiple tournaments can be created and tracked
Backend: GET /api/poker/leaderboard - returns leaderboard
Backend: Full tournament flow (create -> register -> add bots -> start -> action)
Backend: Tournament survives backend restart (verified via my-tournament endpoint)
Frontend: Login and navigate to Casino
Frontend: PKO Poker card visible with NEW badge
Frontend: Create Tournament button creates tournament
Frontend: Success toast 'Tournament created! Waiting for players...'
Frontend: Navigates to poker table /poker/pko_xxx
Frontend: Waiting room displays with player list
Frontend: Add Bot buttons work (+1 Bot, +3 Bots, Fill with Bots)
Frontend: Success toast 'Added 9 AI bot(s)!'
Frontend: Poker table UI displays correctly (green felt, seats, action buttons)
Frontend: Tournament info panel shows Blinds, Prize Pool, Level
Frontend: Table Chat panel present
Frontend: 'You're in a Tournament' screen shows when already registered
Frontend: 'Return to Table' and 'Leave & Refund' buttons work
Frontend: 'Left tournament. Buy-in refunded!' toast on leave
action_items: Add 'await tournament_manager.initialize()' to GET /api/poker/tournaments/{tournament_id} endpoint to fix 404 after restart
Optional: Handle 'bot_added' WebSocket message type in frontend PokerTournament.jsx
critical_code_review_comments: MongoDB persistence is working correctly - tournaments saved to poker_tournaments collection
Player-tournament mapping saved to poker_player_maps collection
Tournaments load from DB on startup via initialize() method
Bug: GET /tournaments/{id} doesn't call initialize() so returns 404 after restart
The my-tournament endpoint works correctly because it calls initialize()
seed_data_creation: Multiple test tournaments created during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: MongoDB persistence for PKO Poker is working. Key findings: 1) Tournaments persist in poker_tournaments collection, 2) Player mappings in poker_player_maps collection, 3) Bug: GET /tournaments/{id} needs initialize() call to work after restart, 4) Frontend fully functional - create, register, add bots, leave all work, 5) WebSocket connection works for real-time updates.
